name: Lower Columbia Longshoremen
description_short: Lower Columbia Longshoremen's Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving union members and families since 1954, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
Lower Columbia Longshoremen's Federal Credit Union (LCLFCU) was established in 1954 with a mission to create financial solutions specifically for union members and their families. Based in Longview, Washington, the credit union operates as a cooperative financial institution, meaning members are both customers and partial owners. This membership model has shaped their product development and service philosophy for nearly 70 years.

LCLFCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, auto loans (new and used vehicles), RV and boat financing (up to 12 years), real estate/mortgage loans, VISA credit cards, cashier's checks, notary services, and signature guarantees. They provide both a Classic VISA with a fixed 9.9% rate and a newer Signature VISA with rewards points redeemable for cash, statement credits, or travel. Members can access accounts through free online banking, a mobile app available on Apple and Google platforms, online bill pay, and a CO-OP Network for ATM access.

What distinguishes LCLFCU is their specialized focus on union members and emphasis on accessibility. They offer Credit Union Direct Lending (CUDL) allowing vehicle financing to be completed at car dealerships, and they provide family membership options to extend eligibility beyond individual union members. The organization maintains expanded lobby hours, drive-up windows, night deposit boxes, and a Golden Futures Club for seniors. Their low fixed VISA rate of 9.9% is competitive, and auto loans come without the manufacturer restrictions or hidden fees often associated with dealer financing.

The primary limitation is membership eligibility—LCLFCU is primarily designed for union members, which excludes the general public. As a regional credit union, their product range, while solid, is more limited than large national banks. The website provides limited information about savings account rates, CD terms, loan APRs beyond the VISA card, or specific real estate loan terms, making it difficult to assess competitive positioning without direct contact.
services: Auto loans for new and used vehicles with Credit Union Direct Lending (CUDL)
RV and boat financing up to 12 years
Real estate loans including 97% equity loans, second mortgages, and undeveloped property
VISA Signature Rewards credit card with points redemption
Classic VISA credit card at fixed 9.9% rate
Free online banking and bill pay
Debit cards with LCLFCU Card Manager
pros: Fixed 9.9% VISA rate is competitive and clearly stated with no hidden fees
Credit Union Direct Lending (CUDL) allows auto financing to be completed at dealership with credit union rates
RV and boat financing up to 12 years with up to 100% financing available
Free online banking, bill pay, and mobile app with 24/7 customer service access
Extended access options including expanded lobby hours, drive-up window, and night deposit box
Family membership eligibility extends benefits beyond individual union members
CO-OP Network access for expanded ATM and branch access nationwide
Golden Futures Club specifically designed for senior members
cons: Membership restricted to union members and their families, excluding general public
Limited transparency on website regarding savings rates, CD terms, and specific auto/mortgage APRs
Regional credit union with limited physical locations compared to national banks
Website lacks detailed comparison information or product rate sheets for informed decision-making
No mention of payday alternative loans (PALs) or emergency quick-cash products
best_for: Union members seeking a member-owned financial institution with personalized service
Vehicle buyers wanting credit union rates and terms completed directly at auto dealerships
RV and boat enthusiasts needing extended financing terms up to 12 years
Families looking to extend credit union membership to dependents through family membership options
diagnosis: LCLFCU is best for union members and their families seeking a cooperative financial institution with competitive rates, especially for vehicle and RV financing. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—you must be a union member or family of one to qualify; general consumers cannot join this credit union.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Lower Columbia Longshoremen — NCUA-insured credit union in LONGVIEW, WA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
